name: OneNoteConnector
description: Connects to Microsoft OneNote via the Microsoft Graph v1.0 API. List, read, create, update, copy, and delete notebooks, section groups, sections, and pages on behalf of an authenticated Microsoft 365 user.

install_fields.label: Application (Client) ID
install_fields.help: Found in Azure Portal → App registrations → your app → Overview → Application (client) ID.

install_fields.label: Client Secret
install_fields.help: Generate under Azure Portal → App registrations → your app → Certificates & secrets → New client secret. Copy the VALUE, not the ID.

install_fields.label: Azure Tenant ID
install_fields.help: Azure AD tenant. Use 'common' for multi-tenant apps, 'organizations' for any work/school account, or a tenant GUID/domain for single-tenant apps. NOT the same as your Shielva tenant_id.

install_fields.label: OAuth2 Redirect URI
install_fields.help: The redirect URI registered in your Azure app. Must match EXACTLY what is configured in Azure Portal → App registrations → your app → Authentication.

install_fields.label: OAuth2 Scopes
install_fields.help: Space-separated Microsoft Graph delegated permissions. 'offline_access' is required to receive a refresh token.

install_fields.label: OAuth2 Authorization URL
install_fields.help: Microsoft identity platform authorization endpoint. Swap 'common' with your tenant ID for single-tenant.

install_fields.label: OAuth2 Token URL
install_fields.help: Microsoft identity platform token endpoint. Swap 'common' with your tenant ID for single-tenant.

install_fields.label: Microsoft Graph OneNote Base URL
install_fields.help: Base URL for the Microsoft Graph OneNote API. Leave blank to use the per-user /me/onenote root.

install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)
install_fields.help: Soft cap on outbound Graph requests per minute. Graph enforces its own throttling; this guards against client-side bursts.

apis.name: Install Connector
apis.description: Validates client_id and client_secret and persists install-time config. Call before OAuth authorization.

apis.name: Authorize (OAuth Code Exchange)
apis.description: Exchanges an OAuth2 authorization code for access + refresh tokens via the Microsoft identity token endpoint.

apis.name: Health Check
apis.description: Verifies Microsoft Graph (OneNote) reachability by listing one notebook via GET /me/onenote/notebooks?$top=1.

apis.name: List Notebooks
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/notebooks — list OneNote notebooks for the signed-in user. Supports $top, $skip, $filter, $orderby.

apis.name: Get Notebook
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/notebooks/{id} — fetch a single notebook by ID.

apis.name: Create Notebook
apis.description: POST /me/onenote/notebooks — create a new notebook with the given display name.

apis.name: List Sections
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/notebooks/{id}/sections or /me/onenote/sections — list sections, optionally scoped to a notebook.

apis.name: Get Section
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/sections/{id} — fetch a single section by ID.

apis.name: Create Section
apis.description: POST /me/onenote/notebooks/{id}/sections — create a section under a notebook.

apis.name: List Pages
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/sections/{id}/pages or /me/onenote/pages — list pages, optionally with $filter or $search.

apis.name: Get Page Content
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/pages/{id}/content — returns the raw XHTML body of the page as a string.

apis.name: Create Page
apis.description: POST /me/onenote/sections/{id}/pages — create a page from a raw XHTML body. Content-Type defaults to application/xhtml+xml (NOT application/json).

apis.name: Update Page
apis.description: PATCH /me/onenote/pages/{id}/content — apply a JSON array of update commands (append, insert, replace, prepend).

apis.name: Copy Page to Section
apis.description: POST /me/onenote/pages/{id}/copyToSection — copy a page to another section. Returns an async operation handle (202 Accepted).

apis.name: Search Pages
apis.description: GET /me/onenote/pages?$search=… — full-text search across all OneNote pages the user can access.
